top_categories.workout_context: GFNY Bremen's flat forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 69 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Bremen's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
When someone attacks on flat, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Bremen, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
69 miles on flat burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
GFNY Bremen's flat rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Bremen. Train it until it feels boring.
At GFNY Bremen, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what GFNY Bremen's flat actually does to you.
Smooth pedaling at 69 miles saves muscle. Bad technique at mile 10 is invisible. At mile 80 it's a cramp.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Bremen’s 69 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Bremen requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on flat to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 69 miles of flat in Bremen, Germany.
